comments.author: @WhereonEarth4K
comments.text:
👇 Everything we curated for this Suez Canal episode. Books, history, cookbooks, travel resources.

*📚 BROWSE THE SUEZ CANAL COLLECTION*
  ◦ Every book, cookbook, and cultural item we curated for this episode → https://amzn.to/4fcK7DL

*🛍 SEE OUR FULL W.O.E. STOREFRONT*
  ◦ Every destination Collection in one place → https://www.amazon.com/shop/whereonearth

*🎟 TOURS AND EXPERIENCES IN EGYPT*
  ◦ Cairo, Nile, pyramids, and Sinai tours → https://shorturl.at/x6GqA
  ◦ Browse all Egypt experiences → https://shorturl.at/c6VC8

*📖 PLAN YOUR EGYPT TRIP*
  ◦ Lonely Planet Egyptian Arabic Phrasebook and Dictionary → https://amzn.to/43CIKqM

*📚 GO DEEPER INTO THE SUEZ CANAL*
  ◦ Parting the Desert, the definitive history of how the canal was built → https://amzn.to/4vhNBcO
  ◦ Blood and Sand, hour-by-hour account of the 1956 Suez Crisis → https://amzn.to/4uESFbl
  ◦ Palace Walk, opening volume of Mahfouz's Nobel-winning Cairo Trilogy → https://amzn.to/49ugsSQ

*🍲 BRING EGYPT HOME*
  ◦ My Egyptian Grandmother's Kitchen, Gourmand World Cookbook Award winner → https://amzn.to/4dSLN2T
  ◦ Treasures of Egypt, National Geographic's legacy photography volume → https://amzn.to/49ZoDGQ

comments.author: @mikekl11
comments.text: You make a Egypt a victim.  Like every Arab country they have terrible dictatorial rulers that don't give a damn about the people they rule.  If Israel would have ruled over the Suez canal, the area would be thriving and rich.

comments.author: @EpicEarthYTB
comments.text: Fascinating overview of the Suez Canal’s history! It’s incredible how such a narrow waterway remains one of the most critical points for global trade.
